summary: Backend regression for VibeRidez escrow hardening + rider history + Solana deposit panel iteration. Extended /app/backend/tests/test_iter_global_vibez_dsg_2026_01.py from 13 → 21 tests; all 21 pass against the public preview URL. Coverage: escrow flow (pending_earned on match, daily/total only on complete), ride_id UUID in /ridez/request response, idempotent /ridez/complete, /ridez/complete 404 on unknown ride_id, driver flips BUSY → AVAILABLE in /api/ridez/active-drivers after completion, /ridez/my-history 401 unauthenticated, /ridez/my-history 200 with Bearer auth showing the just-completed ride (status='completed', rating=5, rider_id == authenticated user_id), /api/uber/* still 404 (subsystem removed), and full regression of crypto-payments (SOL+BTC deposits, platform-receive-wallet) and /vibe-suites/me/balance.
critical_code_review_comments: vibe_ridez_dispatch.py /ridez/complete: only the original `active_ride_id` driver is flipped back to AVAILABLE, but if a driver somehow completes a stale ride (active_ride_id mismatch) they will stay BUSY forever. Consider always resetting status when ride.driver_id == driver and the ride was in_progress, regardless of active_ride_id pointer.

vibe_ridez_dispatch.py /ridez/complete: no auth gating — anyone with a ride_id can mark a ride complete and trigger a payout. Add a check that the caller is either the rider (rider_id matches authenticated user) or the assigned driver. Currently this is exploitable if a ride_id is leaked.

vibe_ridez_dispatch.py /ridez/complete: pending_earned subtraction uses max(0, ...) — masks accounting bugs. If a driver's pending bucket is ever desynced from history rows, the credit will silently double-spend on the daily/total side. Recommend explicit assert/log instead of a clamp.

vibe_ridez_dispatch.py /ridez/my-history: only filters on rider_id == user.user_id. Riders who matched as a `virtual` test user (anonymous rider_id) will see nothing. Acceptable but worth documenting in the rider UI ('only rides taken while signed in are tracked').

vibe_ridez_dispatch.py: in-memory _DRIVERS dict still resets on backend restart — pending_earned escrow can be lost across restarts even though the history row in Mongo still reads in_progress. Consider persisting driver state or replaying outstanding history rows on startup.

vibe_ridez_dispatch.py /ridez/request: accept-then-cancel abuse vector is now mitigated by the escrow gate (rider/driver must call /complete to release funds), which is the explicit goal of this iteration ✅.

Test suite: in-memory _DRIVERS persists across pytest runs against the live preview backend, so prior-run TEST_drv records can out-compete a new test driver in the matcher. Mitigated in this iteration by using uuid-derived unique lat/lon per test run (rider_lat ≈ 10/30/50 + jitter).

success_rate.frontend: skipped per E1 instruction (preview ingress 502s on 38MB webpack-dev bundle)

test_credentials: Used /api/auth/demo-login (auto-provisions a guest session). johnnyh3611@gmail.com / FreshStart2026! is on file in /app/memory/test_credentials.md if needed later.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed in-memory drivers (TEST_drv_*, TEST_drv_loc_*, TEST_drv_avail_*, TEST_drv_hist_*) and a few TEST_-prefixed crypto_deposits + vibe_ridez_history rows in MongoDB. Driver state is in-memory and clears on backend restart; Mongo rows are clearly TEST_-prefixed for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter_global_vibez_dsg_2026_01.py now covers all 10 review-request items (escrow on match, ride_id in response, complete crediting, complete idempotency, complete-unknown 404, my-history 401/200, /api/uber/* 404, crypto + balance regression). Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter_global_vibez_dsg_2026_01.py -v. Frontend remains explicitly out-of-scope per E1's note — DriverDispatch (EARNINGS_PENDING/CREDITED), RiderTracking (Complete + 5-star + history accordion), and SolanaDepositPanel/VibeWallet were NOT exercised here. If the next agent picks up frontend, ingress may 502 on the webpack-dev bundle; consider building the frontend first.
rca of the issue: Initial run failed 1/13 (test_escrow_flow_credits_on_complete_only). Root cause: leftover TEST_drv_* drivers from prior pytest runs were still in the in-memory _DRIVERS dict at the same SF coordinates and beat the freshly-registered driver in the haversine sort. Fix: each test run now derives unique rider/driver lat-lon from a uuid-seeded offset so the new driver is always the closest match. After fix: 21/21 pass.
